sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content:
Pathway ID: papillary_low_risk
Title: Low-Risk Papillary Thyroid Carcinoma
Branching Basis:
- Tumor size ≤4 cm, confined to thyroid, no extrathyroidal extension (ETE), no lymph node metastases (N0/NX), no distant metastases (M0), favorable histology.
Node Criteria: Tumor ≤4 cm, unifocal or multifocal (all foci ≤1 cm), no ETE, no lymph node metastases, postoperative unstimulated thyroglobulin (Tg) <1 ng/mL, negative postoperative ultrasound.
Option: Surgery
Label: Lobectomy (preferred) or total thyroidectomy (category 2B).
Preference: Preferred: Lobectomy. Total thyroidectomy may be considered for bilateral nodularity or patient preference.
Recommendation: Category 2A.
Option: RAI
Label: Not typically recommended.
Preference: Not recommended if all low-risk criteria are present.
Recommendation: Category 2A.

sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content:
Pathway ID: papillary_high_risk
Title: High-Risk Papillary Thyroid Carcinoma
Branching Basis:
- Tumor >4 cm, ETE, lymph node metastases (N1), distant metastases (M1), aggressive histology (tall cell, columnar cell, hobnail, diffuse sclerosing, solid/trabecular), differentiated high-grade carcinoma.
Node Criteria: Any high-risk feature (e.g., T3/T4, N1, M1, aggressive histology).
Option: Surgery
Label: Total thyroidectomy with therapeutic neck dissection for clinically apparent/biopsy-proven disease.
Preference: Preferred.
Recommendation: Category 2A.
Option: RAI
Label: Typically recommended for remnant ablation or adjuvant therapy.
Preference: Recommended.
Recommendation: Category 2A.
Adjuvant: RAI is recommended based on clinicopathologic risk factors (PAP-4). Dose depends on risk (30–50 mCi for ablation, 75–150 mCi for adjuvant, 100–200 mCi for known disease). [THYR-C]

sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content:
Pathway ID: follicular_oncocytic
Title: Follicular and Oncocytic Carcinoma
Branching Basis:
- Histology (follicular or oncocytic), invasion pattern (capsular vs. vascular), tumor size, lymph node involvement (less common in follicular).
Node Criteria: Follicular neoplasm on FNA (Bethesda IV), clinical suspicion of malignancy.
Option: Surgery
Label: Lobectomy/isthmusectomy for diagnostic/therapeutic purposes. Total thyroidectomy if bilateral nodularity or tumor >4 cm.
Preference: Preferred.
Recommendation: Category 2A.
Node Criteria: Confirmed invasive follicular/oncocytic carcinoma (e.g., extensive vascular invasion, gross ETE).
Option: Surgery
Label: Total thyroidectomy with therapeutic neck dissection for involved compartments.
Preference: Preferred.
Recommendation: Category 2A.
Option: RAI
Label: Selective recommendation based on clinicopathologic factors (e.g., large tumor, vascular invasion, N1, Tg levels).
Preference: Individually determined.
Recommendation: Category 2A.
Adjuvant: RAI decisions are based on clinicopathologic factors (FOLL-3, ONC-3). Oncocytic carcinoma is often non-iodine-avid, so FDG-PET may be considered. [FOLL-9, ONC-9]

sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content:
Pathway ID: medullary
Title: Medullary Thyroid Carcinoma
Branching Basis:
- Sporadic vs. hereditary (germline RET PV), RET mutation risk level, presence of pheochromocytoma/hyperparathyroidism.
Node Criteria: Sporadic MTC diagnosed on FNA.
Option: Surgery
Label: Total thyroidectomy with central neck dissection (level VI). Therapeutic lateral neck dissection for clinically involved nodes.
Preference: Preferred.
Recommendation: Category 2A.
Option: Systemic_therapy
Label: For unresectable, recurrent, or metastatic disease.
Preference: Preferred: Selpercatinib for RET-mutant disease; vandetanib or cabozantinib for others.
Recommendation: Category 1 for vandetanib, cabozantinib, and selpercatinib in RET-mutant disease.
Node Criteria: Hereditary MTC with germline RET PV (MEN2A/MEN2B).

nodes.label: Tumor ≤4 cm, unifocal or multifocal (all foci ≤1 cm), no ETE, no lymph node metastases, postoperative unstimulated thyroglobulin (Tg) <1 ng/mL, negative postoperative ultrasound.

Surgery: Lobectomy (preferred) or total thyroidectomy (category 2B). Preference: Preferred: Lobectomy. Total thyroidectomy may be considered for bilateral nodularity or patient preference. Recommendation: Category 2A.
RAI: Not typically recommended. Preference: Not recommended if all low-risk criteria are present. Recommendation: Category 2A.
Any high-risk feature (e.g., T3/T4, N1, M1, aggressive histology).
Surgery: Total thyroidectomy with therapeutic neck dissection for clinically apparent/biopsy-proven disease. Preference: Preferred. Recommendation: Category 2A.
RAI: Typically recommended for remnant ablation or adjuvant therapy. Preference: Recommended. Recommendation: Category 2A.
Follicular neoplasm on FNA (Bethesda IV), clinical suspicion of malignancy.
Surgery: Lobectomy/isthmusectomy for diagnostic/therapeutic purposes. Total thyroidectomy if bilateral nodularity or tumor >4 cm. Preference: Preferred. Recommendation: Category 2A.
Confirmed invasive follicular/oncocytic carcinoma (e.g., extensive vascular invasion, gross ETE).
Surgery: Total thyroidectomy with therapeutic neck dissection for involved compartments. Preference: Preferred. Recommendation: Category 2A.
RAI: Selective recommendation based on clinicopathologic factors (e.g., large tumor, vascular invasion, N1, Tg levels). Preference: Individually determined. Recommendation: Category 2A.

edges.label: If pathology confirms invasive carcinoma (extensive vascular invasion, gross ETE)

nodes.label: Sporadic vs. hereditary (germline RET PV), RET mutation risk level, presence of pheochromocytoma/hyperparathyroidism.
Surgery: Total thyroidectomy with central neck dissection (level VI). Therapeutic lateral neck dissection for clinically involved nodes. Preference: Preferred. Recommendation: Category 2A.
